title: Filesystem Node Read and Stat Flow
interaction_flow: === Node Construction ===
Caller → FSService: node(selector)  // NodeUIDSelector | NodePathSelector | NodeChildSelector | NodeRawEntrySelector
FSService → FSNodeContext: new FSNodeContext({ services, selector, provider, fs })
FSNodeContext → ECMAP: get_fsNodeContext_from_selector(selector)  // dedup check
FSNodeContext → selector: setPropertiesKnownBySelector(this)  // pre-populate uid/path from selector
=== get_optimal_selector ===
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: get_optimal_selector()  // called whenever .selector is accessed
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: iterates SELECTOR_PRIORITY_ORDER: [NodeRawEntrySelector, RootNodeSelector, NodeInternalIDSelector, NodeUIDSelector, NodeChildSelector, NodePathSelector]
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: returns first matching selector type found in selectors_[]
=== fetchEntry (stat) ===
Caller → FSNodeContext: fetchEntry(fetch_entry_options)  // or via exists(), get(key), etc.
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: guard: if fetching !== null, await this.fetching (concurrency dedup)
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: skip if found===true && !force && thumbnail already satisfied
FSNodeContext → PuterFSProvider: stat({ selector: get_optimal_selector(), options, node: this, controls })
=== PuterFSProvider.stat — resource/entry race ===
PuterFSProvider → ResourceService: waitForResource(selector)  // resolves when uid not pending-create
PuterFSProvider → FSEntryController: waitForEntry(node, callback)  // resolves when op queued for uid
note: first of the two to resolve wins; the other is detached
=== PuterFSProvider.stat — fetch from controller ===
alt resource is pending (svc_resource.getResourceInfo(uid) truthy)
PuterFSProvider → FSEntryController: get(maybe_uid, options)  // scans currentState+deferredState queues then DB
FSEntryController → Database: findByUID via find() if needed
else
PuterFSProvider → FSEntryController: find(selector, options)
end
=== FSEntryController.find — selector dispatch ===
FSEntryController → FSEntryController: if RootNodeSelector → return selector.entry
FSEntryController → Database: if NodePathSelector → findByPath(path)  // tries direct path lookup first, then iterative segment walk
FSEntryController → Database: if NodeUIDSelector → findByUID(uuid)  // SELECT defaultProperties FROM fsentries WHERE uuid=?
FSEntryController → Database: if NodeInternalIDSelector → findByID(id)  // SELECT ... WHERE id=?
FSEntryController → FSEntryController: if NodeChildSelector → find(selector.parent) recursively, then findNameInParent(parentUuid, name), then find(new NodeInternalIDSelector(id))
=== Back in stat — post-fetch ===
PuterFSProvider → FSNodeContext (via controls): provide_selector(new NodeInternalIDSelector('mysql', entry.id))  // cache internal ID for future calls
PuterFSProvider → FSNodeContext: return entry object (or null if not found)
=== FSNodeContext.fetchEntry — finalize ===
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: if entry null → found=false, entry=false
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: if entry found → found=true; update uid, mysql_id, path, name from entry; Object.assign(this.entry, entry)
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: this.fetching.resolve()  // unblock concurrent callers
=== Stat checks (root / public / appdata) ===
Caller → FSNodeContext: isRoot  // synchronous: this.path === '/'
Caller → FSNodeContext: isUserDirectory()  // fetchEntry(); return !this.entry.parent_uid
Caller → FSNodeContext: isPublic()  // getPathComponents() → components[1]==='Public' AND NOT isUserDirectory()
Caller → FSNodeContext: isAppDataDirectory()  // getPathComponents() → components[1]==='AppData'
FSNodeContext → FSNodeContext: getPathComponents()  // splits this.path or NodePathSelector.value by '/'
=== readdirstat_uuid (optimized readdir) ===
Caller → PuterFSProvider: readdirstat_uuid({ uuid, options })  // requires READDIR_UUID_MODE capability
PuterFSProvider → FSEntryController: get_descendants_full(uuid, options)
FSEntryController → Database: SELECT defaultProperties [+thumbnail] FROM fsentries AS f LEFT JOIN subdomains AS s ON f.id=s.root_dir_id WHERE parent_uid=? ORDER BY f.id
FSEntryController → FSEntryController: collapse rows by id (handle subdomain join dupes), collect subdomains[]
FSEntryController → PuterFSProvider: return raw_entry[]
PuterFSProvider → FSService: for each raw_entry: svc_fs.node(new NodeRawEntrySelector(raw_entry, { found_thumbnail }))
PuterFSProvider → FSNodeContext (each): node.found = true  // mark as already resolved
PuterFSProvider → Caller: return Promise<FSNodeContext[]>
=== readdir (standard, returns UUIDs only) ===
Caller → PuterFSProvider: readdir({ node })
PuterFSProvider → FSNodeContext: node.get('uid')  // may trigger fetchEntry if not cached
PuterFSProvider → FSEntryController: fast_get_direct_descendants(uuid)
FSEntryController → Database: SELECT uuid FROM fsentries WHERE parent_uid=?  // or IS NULL for root
FSEntryController → PuterFSProvider: return uuid[]
PuterFSProvider → Caller: return uuid[]  // caller must call svc_fs.node() for each to get FSNodeContext
key_relationships: FSNodeContext depends_on PuterFSProvider (via this.provider.stat / get_recursive_size / directory_has_name)
FSNodeContext uses ECMAP (EntityContextMap) for selector deduplication across the request context
FSNodeContext uses selector hierarchy: NodeRawEntrySelector > RootNodeSelector > NodeInternalIDSelector > NodeUIDSelector > NodeChildSelector > NodePathSelector

selector_types.NodeRawEntrySelector: Wraps a raw DB row — bypasses DB fetch entirely; stat() converts to NodeUIDSelector
selector_types.RootNodeSelector: Represents virtual root '/'; find() returns selector.entry directly
selector_types.NodeInternalIDSelector: MySQL row ID; findByID() — fastest DB lookup
selector_types.NodeChildSelector: parent_selector + child_name; resolved recursively via find(parent) then findNameInParent()
selector_types.NodePathSelector: Full path string; findByPath() — tries direct path column first, falls back to segment walk

important_notes: fetchEntry() is idempotent and concurrency-safe via this.fetching TeePromise guard
stat() races waitForResource vs waitForEntry to handle nodes pending DB insert
controls.provide_selector() feeds NodeInternalIDSelector back into FSNodeContext.selectors_ for caching
readdirstat_uuid returns Promise<FSNode[]> (full nodes); readdir returns uuid[] (shallow)
isPublic/isAppDataDirectory rely on path component position [1], so path must be resolved first
FSEntryController batches writes via enqueue_/exec_ but all reads go directly to db.read/tryHardRead
